Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Citizenship Act
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Citizenship Act
Identification.BillSponsor: MINISTER OF IMMIGRATION, REFUGEES AND CITIZENSHIP

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Citizenship Act to include, in the Oath or Affirmation of Citizenship, a solemn promise to respect the Aboriginal and treaty rights of First Nations, Inuit and MÃ©tis peoples.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: The schedule to the Citizenship Act is replaced by the schedule set out in the schedule to this Act.
This Act comes into force on the day after the day on which it receives royal assent.

Schedule.Oath: I swear (or affirm) that I will be faithful and bear true allegiance to Her Majesty Queen Elizabeth the Second, Queen of Canada, Her Heirs and Successors, and that I will faithfully observe the laws of Canada, including the Constitution, which recognizes and affirms the Aboriginal and treaty rights of First Nations, Inuit and MÃ©tis peoples, and fulfil my duties as a Canadian citizen.
